subject: Re: Warp not seeing all .tif files in a directory
body:
Hi Andres and Dimitry,

We haven't been using it on AWS for a while now (now locally running it!)

Andres - I can't remember exactly if this problem went away or what we did
to fix it. How about trying a sync to a different directory than warp is
watching and then moving it over?

Mike

On Sun, Nov 10, 2019 at 3:31 PM Dimitry Tegunov <tegunov@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Andres,
>
> I agree this is likely AWS-specific. Has anyone else using Warp there
> (Mike?) encountered this issue and found a workaround? It might take me a
> while to get access to an AWS instance to troubleshoot this.
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
>
> On Tuesday, November 5, 2019 at 9:27:11 PM UTC+1, Andres Hernandez wrote:
>>
>> I'm also attaching the worker crash files.
>>
>> On Tuesday, November 5, 2019 at 3:21:28 PM UTC-5, Andres Hernandez wrote:
>>>
>>> I'm having the same issue. I had it with 1.0.6 and now with 1.0.7. The
>>> tif files stop showing up in WARP and sometimes I get a crash with an
>>> error. After the crashes, I have to delete the xml files and restart
>>> processing from the beginning or it gets stuck at "loading particle
>>> metadata from previous run." I think it is related to aws s3 sync
>>> (multipart file transfers), but it has also happened with a regular sync
>>> command (only one *.tif.* temp file per file).
>>>
>>> Here is the lastcrash.txt, I am also attaching a screenshot of the error.
>>>
>>> System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed pipe.
>>>    at System.IO.__Error.PipeNotOpen()
>>>    at
>>> System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.CheckConnectOperationsServer()
>>>    at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.WaitForConnection()
>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.SendCommand(NamedSerializableObject command)
>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Dispose()
>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Finalize()
>>>    at System.IO.__Error.PipeNotOpen()
>>>    at
>>> System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.CheckConnectOperationsServer()
>>>    at System.IO.Pipes.NamedPipeServerStream.WaitForConnection()
>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.SendCommand(NamedSerializableObject command)
>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Dispose()
>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Finalize()
>>>
>>>
>>>
>>> On Sunday, July 15, 2018 at 3:19:59 PM UTC-4, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>>>
>>>> The XML files are only initialized once some processing results need to
>>>> be stored – not upon discovery. If a file has been successfully discovered,
>>>> it will show up in the UI.
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>> Dimitry
>>>>
>>>> On Sunday, July 15, 2018 at 9:09:31 PM UTC+2, mcia...@umich.edu wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>> There could be something like this happening as the hard drive on AWS
>>>>> that we were using filled up last night, so Warp might have tried to write
>>>>> files that didn't exist.
>>>>>
>>>>> I'll try splitting them to see what happens. Where is the information
>>>>> stored to indicate that it has 'seen' a file? The xml files?
>>>>>
>>>>> Mike
>>>>>
>>>>> On Sunday, July 15, 2018 at 3:07:30 PM UTC-4, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Hi Mike,
>>>>>>
>>>>>> I haven't encountered this issue before. There were some problems in
>>>>>> the past when another process hadn't finished writing a file yet that Warp
>>>>>> would try to read, but that has been fixed. The file discovery thread
>>>>>> shouldn't crash anymore when encountering a problem reading one of the
>>>>>> files. What happens if you split the files in two folders, isolating the
>>>>>> 1128 it doesn't see?
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Cheers,
>>>>>> Dimitry
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> On Sunday, July 15, 2018 at 8:33:21 PM UTC+2, mcia...@umich.edu
>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Hi Dimitry,
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> We have noticed that the Warp directory being processed has ~2800
>>>>>>> movies, but the total listing of movies in the "Overview" tab only
>>>>>>> totals 1,672. Is there a way to force the program to double check for the
>>>>>>> presence of additional files? I've tried stopping, exiting, restarting but
>>>>>>> it comes back to the same number.  Have you seen this before?
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Mike
>>>>>>>
>>>>>> --

> .
>

_________________________
Michael A. Cianfrocco, Ph.D.
Assistant Professor, Department of Biological Chemistry
Research Assistant Professor, Life Sciences Institute
University of Michigan
Pronouns: he, him, his
